title: Lazy Sunday White Wine Bread
description: A diabetic-friendly white wine bread recipe that's smoky and crumbly, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning snack or breakfast.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: Instant is fine, but activate before use

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup unsalted butter, softened
ingredients.notes: Use lower fat options for diabetic-friendly version

ingredients.name: 3/4 cup white granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: Reduced to ensure not too high in carbs

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt. In another bowl, mix together softened butter, sugar, and eggs.
Stir in the dry ingredients followed by white wine. Add smoked paprika for an extra smoky flavor if desired.
Mix until just combined to avoid overworking the dough which can make it tough.
Transfer the dough to a floured surface and knead briefly (about 2-3 minutes).
Place in a greased loaf pan, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise for about 45 minutes or until doubled in size.
Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) again. Bake the bread for approximately 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown and firm to touch.
Let it cool slightly before slicing.
